hypothesis: If retrosynthesis models are trained on USPTO-retro splits with skewed template frequencies and limited template/molecular diversity (narrow split), then their top-k and round-trip accuracies will be high on in-distribution tests (USPTO-retro and Pistachio ID) but drop markedly on Pistachio OOD and structurally dissimilar products, because the models memorize frequent templates and scaffold patterns rather than learning generalizable disconnection rules.
